LanguageData.USAC.Terms.Title: TERMS_OF_SERVICE // User Service Agreement
LanguageData.USAC.Terms.Content:
USAC Trade Terminal Use and Service Master Agreement
This agreement is entered into by the user (hereinafter referred to as the "User") and United.Stellar.Armament.Company (hereinafter referred to as "USAC" or the "Company") and is legally binding. By voluntarily connecting to or using the Company's communication segments, system ports, and logistics response chains (collectively, the "Service"), the User is deemed to have read, understood, and unconditionally agreed to be bound by all terms of this agreement and any subsequent amendments.
1. Data Collection and Privacy Notice 1.1 Authorization for data collection. The User agrees that, as a necessary condition for USAC to provide and optimize the Service, the Company will continuously collect operational data through its telemetry network, including but not limited to system logs, interaction records, and real-time random sampling of carbon-based vital signs, environmental media, and genetic fragments within the User's defended area (collectively, the "Preliminary Telemetry Data"). 1.2 Ownership of data and derivatives. All physical samples, analytical models, and related intellectual property generated from the Preliminary Telemetry Data are exclusively acquired and fully owned by USAC. The User hereby grants USAC complete, irrevocable, royalty-free rights covering all star sectors involved in the Company's services.
2. Service Availability and Force Majeure 2.1 Service capability limits. USAC will make commercially reasonable efforts to maintain the normal operation of communication and logistics services. The Company bears no liability for breach or culpa in contrahendo for degraded transmission or dispatch caused by objective factors not directly attributable to the Company, including abnormal deep-space physical constants, orbital occlusion, or equipment overload. 2.2 Asset recovery rights under extreme circumstances. In the event of extreme financial restructuring or the withdrawal of strategic defense systems, USAC reserves the right, without prior notice, to forcibly recover and reallocate armed facilities and resources that have been delivered but not yet actually consumed, in order to preserve baseline redundancy and the Company's overall interests. The User may not raise claims or allege breach on this basis.
3. Shipment and Delivery Terms 3.1 Delivery of routine materials and personnel. For personnel allotments and non-construction materials, USAC will use orbital drop-pod modules as the standard delivery vehicle according to operational conditions. Landing coordinates are automatically determined from preliminary telemetry data at high-orbit nodes, and the Company makes no guarantee regarding minor deviations or absolute precision at the final landing point. 3.2 Orbital deployment of structural goods. For all defensive arrays, prefabricated building units, and equivalent structural facilities under this agreement (collectively, the "Facility Goods"), regardless of size or mass, USAC will uniformly require vertical overhead delivery with or without damping by means of an orbital transport clamp. 3.3 Derivative impacts of delivery and waiver of liability. The User understands and acknowledges that, due to the objective engineering characteristics of the orbital ballistic delivery methods and clamp anchoring technologies described above, the delivery process necessarily includes the following physical limitations: a. Incidental environmental effects. During logistics execution, object touchdown, and carrier disengagement, including clamp release, it is unavoidable that certain levels of crustal settlement, structural stress propagation, debris projection, and thermal discharge (collectively, the "Incidental Environmental Effects") will occur. b. Disclaimer for physical damage and consequential loss. To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, USAC and its carriers bear no responsibility for repair, restoration, or monetary compensation for direct or indirect consequences arising from logistics execution, Incidental Environmental Effects, or insufficient bearing capacity at the landing site, including accidental injury or death of personnel or livestock, destruction or loss of existing structures, and depletion of natural resources, including vegetation and mineral deposits. All explicit and implicit risks resulting from delivery are borne solely by the User. c. Transfer of identified assets. For all delivered entities equipped with autonomous defense protocols or armed identification logic, including defensive vehicles and biochemical employees, the operational risk and legal responsibility transfer to the receiving party the moment they fully leave USAC's near-orbit control ("onshore transfer"). Losses within the User's base or friendly-fire incidents caused by misidentification or hostile-response activation shall be deemed the responsibility of the receiving party's on-site deployment and maintenance management, and USAC will not entertain any related claims.
4. Settlement Rules and Default Handling 4.1 Pricing and payment. All service functions are governed by preset billing rules, and the User must pay the full listed price shown by the system in approved tokens. 4.2 Overdue settlement and payment in kind. If the User fails to make full repayment within the grace period, USAC may initiate non-judicial compulsory subrogation procedures. To recover overdue principal and collection costs, USAC may, without interference from local injunction procedures, freeze and forcibly remove transferable physical assets within the User's effective jurisdiction, including but not limited to strategic precious-metal reserves, qualified biological labor and tissues, derivative organ sets, and recoverable scrap mechanical units.
5. Compliance Commitments and Use Restrictions During the validity period of the Service authorization, the User is expressly prohibited from performing or assisting any third party in performing the following acts: 5.1 Prohibited acts. a. Disseminating or introducing unverified false identity protocols to evade authentication and credit evaluation. b. Reverse engineering or disassembling contracted components to penetrate or sabotage the network core layer. c. Obstructing, resisting, or responding with kinetic force to Company line inspectors, damage assessment teams, or frontline debt-collection squads, regardless of whether those personnel are targeting the User directly. 5.2 Termination for breach. Any obvious violation of the compliance requirements above constitutes a material and incurable breach of this agreement. USAC may immediately terminate communication and service privileges unilaterally and directly initiate asset preservation procedures and armed liquidation orders under Section 4.2.
6. Liability Cap and Final Interpretation 6.1 Exclusive compensation cap. Unless the parties separately execute a supplemental property exemption agreement, the maximum cumulative liability of USAC and its co-signing carriers for any direct or indirect physical damage, friendly-fire incident, or total facility collapse arising from the use or inability to use the Service shall be limited to refunding the exact amount actually paid by the User for the service currently in dispute. The Company expressly disclaims any legal compensation for unknown losses, consequential damages, prospective value loss, goodwill impairment, or other indirect damages. 6.2 Sole right of interpretation. USAC reserves the final right of interpretation regarding any textual ambiguity or dispute over the application of this agreement to specific circumstances, and that interpretation shall be binding on the User.
LanguageData.USAC.Terms.DeclineMessage: [USAC] You declined the user agreement and cannot use the USAC trade terminal.
LanguageData.USAC.Settings.EnableUSACTerminal: Enable USAC trade terminal and agreement popup
LanguageData.USAC.Settings.EnableUSACTerminalDesc: If enabled, the vanilla comms console will be intercepted and the USAC agreement will appear before the first trade. If you decline the agreement in-game, this option will be automatically unchecked. If disabled, the game will revert to and permanently use the default vanilla or third-party trading interface.
